claim: The explorer's claim about comprehensive boundary audit implementation is confirmed, but a critical gap exists: the system relies only on prompt instructions to prevent full-tool agents from mutating project files, with no enforcement at the tool-restriction level.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot captures SHA-256 digests of all project files except output_dir before agent execution begins
evidence.why: Establishes pre-execution baseline for detecting mutations

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit compares snapshots and populates result.boundary with project_mutation status
evidence.why: Detects mutations that occurred during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force prd_ready=false regardless of agent results
evidence.why: Core enforcement mechanism that blocks PRD readiness on mutations

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full, sets claude_no_session_persistence=true, and clears claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools to empty strings
evidence.why: Confirms that tool restrictions are not set at the provider level; boundary enforcement relies only on prompt instructions

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes explicit boundary rule but no technical enforcement: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files'
evidence.why: Shows boundary enforcement is contractual (prompt-based) not technical (tool-restriction based)

evidence.summary: executionConfig conditionally sets agent_tool_profile to 'full' when allowFullAgentTools=true and agentToolProfile includes 'full', 'all', 'write', or 'execution'
evidence.why: Full tool profile grants actual Write/Edit tool access while relying only on prompt instructions to prevent misuse

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation uses spawnProviderPrompt mock that directly writes unexpected-project-write.txt
evidence.why: Proves boundary detection works, but mutation is injected by test harness not by real agent tools

evidence.summary: All tests use spawnProviderPrompt mock option instead of real Claude provider invocation
evidence.why: Confirms no evidence of real full-tool Claude agent testing

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime uses options.spawnProviderPrompt || defaultSpawnProviderPrompt, defaulting to real provider invocation
evidence.why: Real providers can be invoked but no tests demonstrate this with full-tool agents

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude command with optional --allowedTools flag but evidence-dispatch clears this to empty string
evidence.why: Technical tool restriction capability exists but is intentionally not used for evidence agents

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary declares project_mutation=forbidden and allowed_write_roots as output_dir only
evidence.why: Policy boundary exists but is enforced post-execution, not pre-execution at tool level

assumptions: Real full-tool Claude agents will respect prompt-based boundary rules despite having Write/Edit tool access
Post-execution boundary audit can detect all possible mutations before they cause damage
Agents cannot bypass boundary detection by modifying files then reverting them before audit completes
Claude provider will honor agent_tool_profile setting without additional tool restrictions
risks: Full-tool agents with Write/Edit access could mutate project files before boundary audit runs, relying only on prompt compliance
Agents running in full tool profile have technical capability to modify files despite boundary rules in prompts
Post-execution detection means damage occurs before violation is discovered
No evidence that real Claude agents in full tool mode will respect contractual boundary rules
Tool restrictions (claude_allowed_tools) are deliberately cleared, removing technical enforcement layer
missing: Integration tests with actual Claude provider (not spawnProviderPrompt mock) using full tool profile
Evidence of boundary enforcement holding against real full-tool Claude agents in production
Documentation on whether Claude provider respects agent_tool_profile without explicit --allowedTools
Test scenarios where full-tool agents attempt mutations and are blocked by something other than post-execution audit
Analysis of window between agent execution start and boundary snapshot completion for race conditions

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is confirmed and well-tested for detection, but a critical architectural gap exists: enforcement is post-execution (detect-and-block) not pre-execution (prevent). Full-tool agents have actual Write/Edit access and rely only on prompt instructions to avoid using them. This is a security-relevant finding because the claim suggests comprehensive protection when in fact the system has no technical prevention layer, only detection after the fact.
